title: Add M3 relationship graph and social events
summary: Add relationship edges and ordinary social event facts for M3 ordinary-life consequences.
acceptance: Relationship graph stores focused ordinary-life edges such as kinship care trust gratitude and resentment with stable ids and integer values
Social event facts are structured data generated from ordinary-life events and can feed thoughts/mood without becoming text-only content
Relationship queries use bounded indexed candidate lanes and stable ordering
Scenario-facing explanations distinguish relationship fact source trust and recent events
Focused tests cover deterministic edge updates event generation candidate caps serialization shape and replay-stable hashes

history.details: Created by WM-0045 M3 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

Implemented focused M3 RelationshipGraphStore with stable integer edges, structured social event facts, bounded indexed edge/event candidate lanes, explanation traces, deterministic snapshots/hashes, docs and tests; typecheck, focused Vitest, supported sim-core wrapper, taskctl validate, git diff --check, and pnpm quality pass.

changes_requested: High: createExplanation walks an unbounded actor/lane event bucket while filtering target, outside the ADR social candidate cap; Medium: explanation sourceEventId/sourceTick are edge-wide and can point to a different lane than the trust fact being explained; Low: invalid event kind is not runtime-rejected despite the structured reason.

Repaired WM-0055 review findings: bounded createExplanation traversal with cap/visited metrics and cap-hit trace evidence, lane-specific relationship source metadata so trust explanations cite trust sources, and runtime rejection for malformed social event kinds. Added regressions; typecheck, focused Vitest, supported sim-core wrapper, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check, and pnpm quality pass.
verified: No blocking findings; repaired bounded explanation traversal, trust-lane source reporting, and malformed event-kind rejection verified; requested checks pass.

objective: Create the relationship/social state needed for M3 scenario consequences without Chronicle or M4 social gameplay.
forbiddenPaths: Chronicle evidence town rules anomaly crisis or director systems
broad dialogue content or localization production
global social graph scans in hot paths
benchmarkImpact: Records relationship edge counts social event counts candidate caps and thought/mood event fanout; baseline updates wait for WM-0059.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; gameplay-designer may inspect semantics and M4 boundary.
rollbackModel: Revert relationship/social code tests docs and report while preserving mood/thought owner stores.
ownerGate: Keep relationship facts structured and bounded
Do not implement Chronicle evidence or town-rule social gameplay
Use indexed queries for social candidates
Block on save protocol schema dependency UI authority or M4 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Relationship state ownership and social event simulation are outside Spark scope.

review.summary: No blocking findings; repaired bounded explanation traversal, trust-lane source reporting, and malformed event-kind rejection verified; requested checks pass.
